title: Diabetes Monitoring
description: Organize HbA1c, glucose, and related lab panels into a timeline for recurring diabetes care conversations.

seo.title: Diabetes Lab Monitoring with MedLens+
seo.description: Track HbA1c and glucose over time with ranges from your actual reports. Prep for endocrinology visits with an organized lab history.

persona: Adult managing type 2 diabetes with quarterly lab work

sections.heading: Your endocrinologist asks about your A1c trend…
paragraphs: You had labs in January, April, and again last week. Each visit produced a new PDF, but the story is in how HbA1c moved from 6.8% to 7.1% to 7.2% — plus what happened to fasting glucose and lipids along the way.
MedLens+ organizes these reports into one timeline so you're not scrolling through email attachments five minutes before your appointment.

sections.heading: Track what your care team monitors
paragraphs: Upload reports from any laboratory and view HbA1c, glucose, and related markers chronologically. Reference ranges come from your uploaded documents, not generic internet tables.
We highlight changes and flag incomplete panels. We don't interpret whether your diabetes is "well controlled" — that's a clinical judgment your healthcare provider makes.

sections.heading: Walk in with context
paragraphs: Export a visit brief summarizing recent shifts in your lab history and discussion topics tied to your reports. Bring focused questions: "My HbA1c crept up this quarter — is that worth adjusting our plan?"
Your care team interprets results in the context of your full treatment plan. MedLens+ provides organizational clarity; your clinician provides medical judgment.

faq.question: Can MedLens+ tell me if my diabetes is well controlled?
faq.answer: No. We show how your lab values changed over time relative to the reference ranges on your reports. Whether that indicates good control is a clinical judgment your healthcare provider makes.

faq.question: Which diabetes-related tests are supported?
faq.answer: Early access covers HbA1c, fasting glucose, lipid panels, and kidney function markers commonly ordered in diabetes monitoring. We're expanding based on extraction quality and community feedback.

faq.question: Can I share my timeline with my doctor?
faq.answer: Yes. The visit brief exports a focused summary you can bring to appointments or share through your clinician's preferred channel. Your original PDFs remain available as source documents.
